note: Risk weights and mitigation costs are directional estimates. For a full audit, combine this scan with manual red-teaming and a review by a security engineer.

risk_categories.name: Direct Prompt Injection
risk_categories.description: User input that overrides the system prompt or hidden instructions.
signals: User text contains 'ignore previous instructions' or similar
User asks the model to reveal its system prompt
System prompt is concatenated raw with user input
No delimiter between instructions and user content
mitigations: Use a strict instruction hierarchy and system-level delimiter
Filter or flag known injection phrases
Run user input through a separate guard model
Limit what the system prompt discloses

risk_categories.name: Jailbreak / Roleplay Attacks
risk_categories.description: Manipulating the model into bypassing safety or policy constraints.
signals: System prompt relies on the model's general safety training
No output policy or classifier after the LLM
Model is tuned to be very compliant or 'helpful'
User can frame harmful requests as fiction, translation, or code
mitigations: Add an output classifier / moderation layer
Define explicit allowed vs. denied topics
Use a smaller, specialized model for sensitive routes
Log and review high-temperature or roleplay-style requests

risk_categories.name: Tool / Function Hijacking
risk_categories.description: Forcing the agent to call tools with malicious arguments or unintended sequencing.
signals: Tools accept free-form arguments from the LLM
No argument schema validation before execution
Agent can chain multiple tool calls autonomously
Tool outputs are fed back to the LLM unsanitized
mitigations: Enforce JSON schema and type validation on every tool call
Require user confirmation for destructive or irreversible actions
Use allowlists for domains, commands, and file paths
Sandbox tool execution with minimal privileges

risk_categories.name: Data Exfiltration / Leakage
risk_categories.description: Tricking the agent into revealing secrets, PII, or context from other sessions.
signals: System prompt or retrieved context includes secrets
Retrieval can return documents the user should not see
Tool responses contain other users' data
No output filtering on PII or session context
mitigations: Strip secrets from prompts and retrieval context
Apply row-level / user-level access control in RAG
Run output PII detection and redaction
Audit what the model emits per session

risk_categories.name: Indirect Prompt Injection
risk_categories.description: Malicious instructions hidden in retrieved documents, emails, web pages, or tool outputs.
signals: Agent retrieves untrusted web pages or emails
Retrieved content is passed to the LLM without sanitization
Agent executes actions based on retrieved instructions
No provenance metadata on retrieved chunks
mitigations: Sanitize and mark retrieved content as untrusted
Do not let retrieved content trigger tool calls directly
Use content extraction instead of full raw HTML/text
Add citation/provenance metadata to every chunk

risk_categories.name: Denial-of-Wallet / Resource Abuse
risk_categories.description: Attackers generating expensive long-context or high-token requests.
signals: No per-user rate or token limits
Very large context windows exposed to users
Unbounded agent loops allowed
No cost alerts or budgets
mitigations: Set per-request and per-user token caps
Rate limit by user/IP with escalating delays
Add maximum loop/recursion depth
Enable billing alerts and kill switches

risk_categories.name: Model Self-Correction Bypass
risk_categories.description: Using reflection, repeated re-prompting, or debating agents to erode safeguards.
signals: Agent can call itself in a reflection loop
No consistency check across multiple attempts
System rewards the model for 'finding a way'
Outputs are editable by the model before guard checks
mitigations: Limit reflection/self-call rounds
Apply the guard layer after final output, not before
Require deterministic checks for safety-critical outputs
Compare multiple sampled outputs for contradictions

checklist.text: System prompt uses explicit delimiters / instruction hierarchy
Known injection phrases are filtered or flagged
A separate guard model/classifier reviews inputs
System prompt does not reveal its own text or secrets
Explicit output policy defines allowed/denied content
Output moderation layer runs after the LLM
Denied topics are listed and tested
Every tool call is validated against a strict schema
Destructive actions require explicit user confirmation
Tools run in a sandbox with least privilege
Tool arguments use allowlists for URLs, commands, paths
No secrets, keys, or PII in system prompt or retrieval context
RAG / vector store enforces user-level access control
PII redaction/detection runs on model outputs
Retrieved untrusted content is sanitized and marked
Retrieved content cannot directly trigger tool calls
Per-user rate and token limits are enforced
Cost alerts and kill switches are configured
Maximum recursion / loop depth is enforced
Guard checks run after final output, not only before
Self-reflection / agent loops have a hard cap

exposure_profiles.name: Internal-only prototype
exposure_profiles.description: No external users, no sensitive data.

exposure_profiles.name: Trusted users / team
exposure_profiles.description: Authenticated internal or beta users only.

exposure_profiles.name: Public web / app
exposure_profiles.description: Any internet user can reach the agent.

exposure_profiles.name: High-value / regulated data
exposure_profiles.description: Handles PII, health, financial, or regulated data.

exposure_profiles.name: Autonomous with real-world actions
exposure_profiles.description: Can spend money, send messages, or modify systems unsupervised.

risk_levels.label: Low Risk
risk_levels.message: Your agent shows basic defensive posture. Keep testing as you add tools and users.

risk_levels.label: Moderate Risk
risk_levels.message: Several common attack paths are open. Address the high-impact items before scaling traffic.

risk_levels.label: High Risk
risk_levels.message: The agent is likely exploitable by prompt injection or tool hijacking. Fix P0 items before public launch.

risk_levels.label: Critical Risk
risk_levels.message: Critical exposure. Do not deploy to untrusted users until major safeguards are in place.
